Add FastAPI backend with ACLED/GDELT integration and severity scoring (#2)

* Add FastAPI backend with ACLED/GDELT integration and severity scoring

- ACLED API service with OAuth authentication for conflict event data
- GDELT API service for contextual news articles with tone analysis
- Composite severity score algorithm (1-10) weighing deadliness, civilian danger, event frequency, geographic spread, and trend direction

- GET /api/news returns GDELT articles with average tone

fix: add timeout wrappers to prevent recommendation API hangs

- Add withTimeout() helper function with graceful fallback
- Wrap enrichPantryWithNutrition calls (8s timeout)
- Wrap NutritionEnricher.enrichRecipes (10s timeout)
- Add AIRTABLE_ENRICHMENT and NUTRITION_ENRICHMENT timeout constants

were causing 70+ second hangs for 'En casa' and 'Receta Rápida' types.

Now: If enrichment times out, recommendation continues without exact
nutritional data rather than hanging indefinitely.

Add support for the com.microsoft::GatherBlockQuantized contrib operator
which combines gather and block dequantization in a single operation.

Supports:
- Packed data (UINT8 containing 4-bit values) and native sub-byte types (INT4, UINT4)
- 4-bit and 8-bit quantization
- Optional zero_points (packed or unpacked)
- Configurable gather_axis and quantize_axis
- Default zero_point of 2^(bits-1) for unsigned data types

commit.message:
Fix/audit 03 28 auto (#10)

* fix(security): implement token sanitization and Discord reconnection handlers

Problem: Discord tokens could be exposed in logs during authentication errors (ISSUE-001), and bot had no explicit handling for disconnections causing potential hangs (ISSUE-002).

Solution:
- Created TokenSanitizationFilter in utils/filters.py using regex to replace token patterns and sensitive keys with *** in all log records
- Integrated filter to all logger handlers (console and file) preventing token leakage in tracebacks
- Added specific exception handling for discord.LoginFailure and discord.HTTPException in bot/__main__.py
- Implemented on_error(), on_disconnect(), and on_resumed() handlers in PipBot class for explicit reconnection logging

Impact: Critical security vulnerability eliminated - tokens cannot leak via logs even during failures. Discord reconnection stability improved with explicit monitoring and logging.

Refs: ISSUE-001, ISSUE-002

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(raspberry): add systemd resource limits to protect RPi

Problem: pip-bot.service had no memory or CPU limits, risking OOM killer activation and system freeze on Raspberry Pi (ISSUE-008).

Solution: Added systemd resource constraints:
- MemoryLimit=800M: Caps bot at 800MB (leaves buffer for OS on 4GB RPi)
- OOMPolicy=kill: Let systemd kill bot cleanly if OOM occurs (vs kernel OOM killer)
- TasksMax=50: Prevent spawn of excessive threads/processes
- CPUQuota=80%: Prevent bot from consuming all CPU cycles

Impact: Raspberry Pi stability improved - bot cannot exhaust system memory or CPU, protecting other services and filesystem integrity.

Refs: ISSUE-008

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(performance): make psutil calls non-blocking with thread pool executor

Problem: psutil.cpu_percent(interval=1) blocks the Discord event loop for 1 second, causing Discord commands to timeout or lag (ISSUE-009).

Solution:
- Changed psutil.cpu_percent(interval=1) to interval=None for instant non-blocking sampling
- Created new async get_system_status_async() function that uses loop.run_in_executor() to run blocking psutil calls in thread pool
- Updated /status command to await get_system_status_async() instead of synchronous get_system_status()
- Both functions remain available: sync version for tests/utilities, async for Discord cogs

Impact: Discord /status command no longer blocks event loop, eliminating timeout risks and keeping bot responsive to other commands.

Refs: ISSUE-009

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(config): enforce NAS configuration all-or-none validation

Problem: NAS configuration could be partially set (e.g., NAS_HOST without NAS_PASSWORD), leading to silent failures when Phase 2 NAS integration is implemented (ISSUE-006).

Solution:
- Added validation in get_settings() that checks NAS configuration completeness
- If any NAS variable is set, all four (NAS_HOST, NAS_PORT, NAS_USER, NAS_PASSWORD) must be set
- Raises ConfigError with clear message if configuration is incomplete
- Added two test cases to verify partial NAS configurations are rejected

Impact: Configuration errors are caught early at startup, preventing silent Phase 2 NAS integration failures.

Refs: ISSUE-006

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(devops): improve deployment robustness with .env backup and poetry PATH resolution

Problem: Deploy script could overwrite .env with git reset if accidentally committed (ISSUE-018), poetry PATH was hardcoded risking failures when poetry is elsewhere (ISSUE-020), and deployment errors were silently discarded (ISSUE-007).

Solution:
- Added .env backup before git reset and restoration after, preventing configuration loss
- Improved poetry install error reporting by capturing and displaying stderr output
- Updated pip-bot.service to use PATH-based poetry resolution with ExecStartPre validation
- ExecStartPre checks poetry availability before attempting start

Impact: Deployments are safer - .env configuration persists across updates, errors are visible for debugging, and service start is more resilient to poetry installation location variations.

Refs: ISSUE-018, ISSUE-020, ISSUE-007

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cogs): add rate limiting, timeouts, and improved exception handling to commands

Problem: Commands had no rate limiting (spam/DoS risk), no timeouts on Discord API calls (potential hangs), and inconsistent exception handling (ISSUE-003, ISSUE-012, ISSUE-014).

Solution:
- Added @app_commands.checks.cooldown(1, 60) to /ping, /status, and /help commands (1 use per 60 seconds per user)
- Wrapped Discord API calls with asyncio.timeout(): 5s for /ping, 15s for /status, 10s for /help
- Improved exception handling with specific TimeoutError handling and state checks (is_done()) before deferring
- Added user-friendly timeout error messages
- Preserved async behavior to prevent event loop blocking

Impact: Commands are spam-proof, won't hang indefinitely on slow Discord API, and provide better user feedback on errors.

Refs: ISSUE-003, ISSUE-012, ISSUE-014

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(architecture): add service base class, import tests, and .gitignore improvements

Problem: Phase 2+ services had no defined interface (ISSUE-010), no way to detect circular imports early (ISSUE-016), and .env backup files weren't explicitly ignored (ISSUE-011).

Solution:
- Created services/base.py with BaseService ABC defining common interface (initialize, shutdown, health_check, get_status)
- Added tests/test_imports.py with 6 import verification tests to detect circular dependencies
- Updated .gitignore to explicitly include .env* pattern for backup files
- All abstract methods documented with clear contracts for Phase 2+ implementations

Impact: Phase 2+ service implementations have clear interface contract, circular dependencies are caught early in tests, backup files won't accidentally commit.

Refs: ISSUE-010, ISSUE-016, ISSUE-011

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(release): bump version to 1.2.0 and finalize audit fixes

This release includes comprehensive fixes for 16 critical, high, and medium-priority issues from the security and robustness audit.

Summary of fixes across 7 commits:
1. Token sanitization and Discord reconnection handlers (security)
2. Systemd resource limits for RPi protection (reliability)
3. Async/await consistency for psutil calls (performance)
4. NAS configuration validation (config safety)
5. Deployment robustness with .env backup and poetry PATH fixes (devops)
6. Rate limiting, timeouts, and exception handling for commands (user experience)
7. Service base class, import tests, .gitignore improvements (architecture)

Tests: 76 passing (added 6 new import verification tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Mailgun email notifications for contributions and messages

New Mailgun client library (TheDiscDb.Mailgun) for sending email via
the Mailgun v3 API. Conditional registration: uses real service when
Mailgun:ApiKey is configured, no-op NullContributionNotificationService
otherwise.

Email notifications:
- Contribution submitted for review (admin + user confirmation)
- Contribution imported (user notification with link to item)
- Message from user to admin (admin notification)
- Message from admin to user (user notification with messages link)

Email features:
- Branded layout with TheDiscDb logo header and footer
- Markdown rendering in message emails (Markdig with DisableHtml)
- Plain-text fallback auto-generated from HTML
- Open/click tracking enabled
- Custom variables (contribution-id, notification-type) for webhooks
- List-Unsubscribe headers for inbox placement
- Reply-To headers (admin emails reply to user, user emails to admin)
- Brand color #00213F used consistently

Key fixes during development:
- Await notifications before navigation (fire-and-forget killed emails)
- Move notifications into MessageService (single notification source)
- Remove duplicate sends after merge conflict

Includes 3 new unit tests verifying exactly-one notification per message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Create English Explorer kids learning web app

Complete single-page app with 7 screens:
- Home screen with 6 activity cards + hold-to-unlock Parent Zone
- Learn Letters: all 26 A-Z with examples, audio buttons, auto-play, progress dots
- Trace Letters: dual-canvas (guide + draw), touch+mouse, letter picker, encouragement
- Phonics Sounds: letter-sounds grid + word-blending tab with 20 CVC words
- Read Words: 3 levels (CVC/families/sight words), clickable letters, Read-With-Me
- Listening Game: 3 mini-games (Find Letter, Find Sound, Match Word) with lives + stars
- Daily Adventure: date-seeded 9-step path through all activities
- Parent Zone: progress stats, badges (3 milestones), settings, reset

Features:
- SpeechSynthesis API integration with graceful fallback
- localStorage state persistence
- Star reward system with badge milestones (5/10/20 stars)
- Mobile-first responsive design (320px+) with Fredoka One + Nunito fonts
- Fixed top bar + bottom nav (hold-2s for Parent Zone)
- CSS animations: star pop, badge slide, correct flash, wrong shake
- Touch + mouse canvas drawing with coordinate scaling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
